commands.repair_reason: - Manual review is appropriate because the blocker is a protected, security-sensitive policy contract disagreement between runtime source/tests, docs, and reviewer discussion.; Needs attention: The diff adds security/compliance policy checks, and the retention default mismatch can produce incorrect data-handling conformance results.; Review finding: [P1] Align retention evidence with the runtime default — extensions... (sha=898b0a686472c5b3324b665c859f15e94bdb6047)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Policy plugin data-handling conformance rules, check IDs, evidence collection, doctor tests, and policy docs for redaction, telemetry content capture, retention maintenance, and session transcript memory indexing.

PR surface: Source +704, Tests +418, Docs +48. Total +1170 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection reproduces the blocker: the PR head reports omitted `session.maintenance.mode` as `warn`, while current main's resolver and test pin the runtime default to `enforce`. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - Manual review is appropriate because the blocker is a protected, security-sensitive policy contract disagreement between runtime source/tests, docs, and reviewer discussion.
